summary: Epic Alligator>Caution jam, soaring Dark Star, tight The Eleven
blurb: The second set erupts with an explosive Alligator‑Caution sequence, a masterful Dark Star and a driving The Eleven. Jerry’s guitar is in full bloom while Phil’s bass duels, and Bill Kreutzmann delivers a legendary drum solo. The full‑show soundboard capture preserves the band at peak intensity.
review: On August 22, 1968 at the Fillmore West the Dead were firing on all cylinders. After a shaky mono opening of Saint Stephen, the band found its stride in Set 2, launching into a soaring Dark Star that built tension before diving straight into The Eleven’s rhythmic vortex. From there they exploded into an Alligator‑Caution jam that showcases Jerry Garcia’s endless runs and vocal power, while Bill Kreutzmann tears through a drum solo many fans call the best of the era. Pigpen adds perfect organ flourishes on Death Don't Have No Mercy, Phil Lesh delivers aggressive, funk‑tinged bass lines and even a rare extended solo after New Potato Caboose, and the entire ensemble rides a wave of high‑energy improvisation that culminates in a heartfelt Good Night finale. The full‑show Cotsman soundboard recording captures the intensity with clear instrument separation on the second set, making it the definitive listening experience for this legendary night.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are overtly positive, praising the Alligator>Caution jam, Dark Star, The Eleven and Bill's drum solo; a third recording is mixed but still highlights strong playing. Consistent language like 'epic', 'soaring', 'tight' across sources indicates solid, above‑average performance.

best_recording.reason: Full‑show soundboard capture with good overall mix, clear second‑set separation and the only source that includes every standout jam.

key_highlights: Explosive Alligator > Caution sequence with Garcia’s soaring solos
Masterful Dark Star that builds tension before The Eleven transition
Bill Kreutzmann’s legendary drum solo, crystal‑clear on the SBD
Pigpen’s organ embellishments on Death Don't Have No Mercy and powerful Good Night finale

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers endless runs and soaring solos on Alligator, Caution and Dark Star; his tone is described as full bloom and his vocal presence remains strong throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides aggressive, funky bass lines, duels with Jerry during The Eleven and takes a rare extended solo after New Potato Caboose, keeping the low end both tight and adventurous.
band_performance.Pigpen: Pigpen’s organ adds perfect embellishments on Death Don't Have No Mercy, enhancing the song’s gospel‑blues feel.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann goes ‘nuts’ with a crystal‑clear drum solo and drives the high‑energy jams with relentless power.
